mnemonics: P.E.R.S.I.S.T.: (P)odcasts, (E)-books, (R)eports, (S)tudies, (I)nternet Portals, (S)eminars, (T)utorials - a way to PERSIST in learning about e-waste.
keyPoints: Utilize official reports from organizations like the UNEP and Basel Convention for authoritative data.
Online portals from government agencies (e.g., EPA) provide crucial regulatory and guidance information.
Academic journals and industry white papers offer in-depth technical and scientific research findings.
commonMistakes: Relying solely on general web searches without verifying the credibility of the source (e.g., using Wikipedia as a primary source).
Overlooking the practical guidance offered by manufacturer and recycler take-back programs and websites.
Ignoring the difference between advocacy group perspectives and peer-reviewed scientific data.
